messages.content:
Être bénévole pendant l’épidémie du coronavirus du Covid — 19 :

Je donne des conseils pour l’_______________ .


insertion professionnelle

engagement professionnel ?
engagement bénévole ?
qu'est ce qu'une Activité non rétribuée et librement choisie qui s'exerce en général au sein d'une institution sans but lucratif ?

Il s'agit d'une définition du bénévolat. Le bénévolat consiste en effet à consacrer librement et gratuitement son temps et ses compétences au service d'une cause ou d'une organisation à but non lucratif. L'objectif est de contribuer à l'amélioration de la vie de la communauté ou de la société dans son ensemble. Le bénévolat peut prendre différentes formes, comme le volontariat dans une association, l'aide aux personnes en difficulté ou encore la participation à des actions de solidarité locale.
Le Panthéon est un symbole de quoi ?

Le Panthéon est un symbole de la Révolution française et de l'idée de liberté, d'égalité et de fraternité qui en a découlé. Il a été construit par l'architecte Soufflot à la demande de Louis XV en 1755 pour être une église dédiée à Sainte Geneviève, mais il fut transformé en mausolée national durant la Révolution française. Cette transformation visait à honorer les grands hommes et femmes qui avaient contribué à l'histoire de la France, indépendamment de leur religion ou de leur origine sociale. Ainsi, le Panthéon accueille aujourd'hui les restes de personnalités comme Voltaire, Rousseau, Victor Hugo, Marie Curie, Jean Jaurès ou encore Simone Veil. Le Panthéon est également un monument emblématique de l'architecture classique française du XVIIIe siècle.
le panthéon est il un symbole de la république ?

Oui, effectivement, le Panthéon est un symbole de la République en France. Initialement église consacrée à Sainte-Geneviève, le bâtiment est devenu un lieu de sépulture pour les grands hommes de la Nation lors de la Révolution française. Depuis l'instauration de la Troisième République en 1871, le Panthéon a accueilli les restes de personnalités qui ont marqué l'histoire de France, tout en conservant son rôle de monument emblématique de l'architecture classique française. En tant que lieu de sépulture des personnalités françaises les plus remarquables, le Panthéon symbolise ainsi la grandeur de la Nation et l'importance de ses valeurs, telles que la liberté, l'égalité et la fraternité.
wrong or right, ‘Parliament’ is a royal institution .

Wrong. The Parliament is not a royal institution, but rather a governing body in the United Kingdom and other countries modeled after the British system of government. The Parliament is made up of elected representatives, including members of the House of Commons and the House of Lords, who are responsible for making and amending laws, scrutinizing the actions of the government, and discussing issues of national importance. While the Parliament has some historical ties to the monarchy, it is a separate and distinct institution that holds its own powers and responsibilities.
Who's King George

There were six British monarchs who ruled under the name of George. Here is a brief overview:

1. George I (r. 1714-1727) - First Hanoverian king of Great Britain, succeeded Queen Anne.

2. George II (r. 1727-1760) - Son of George I, extended the Hanoverian dynasty in Great Britain.

3. George III (r. 1760-1820) - Grandson of George II, famous for his long reign and role in the American Revolution.

4. George IV (r. 1820-1830) - Eldest son of George III, known for his extravagant lifestyle and patronage of the arts.

5. William IV (r. 1830-1837) - Brother of George IV, known for his support of electoral reform and abolition of slavery.

6. George V (r. 1910-1936) - Grandson of Queen Victoria, ruled during World War I and the rise of the Labour Party.

In summary, King George refers to any one of the six British monarchs who ruled under that name.
wrong or right ? The ‘premiership’ illustrates the rise in power of the monarchy.

Wrong. The "premiership" actually refers to the position of the Prime Minister, the head of the government in the United Kingdom. The Prime Minister has significantly more power in the UK government than the monarch, particularly after the development of the modern constitutional monarchy in the 19th century. The premiership is not a symbol of the rise of power of the monarchy, but rather a symbol of the democratic process and the evolution of British governance over time.
wrong or right ? As from this time royal music would be very showy and spectacular.

Wrong. The phrasing of the statement is unclear, but if you are referring to a specific time period or event, it would be helpful to provide more context. However, in general, it is not accurate to say that royal music is always showy and spectacular.

Throughout history, the music written for royalty has varied greatly in style and purpose. While some pieces may have been written to celebrate grand and ostentatious events, there are many examples of royal music that are understated, solemn or reflective. Moreover, the specific style of music favored by royalty has changed over time, reflecting wider cultural trends and preferences. For example, during the Baroque period, many European monarchies favored elaborate and ornate music, while in more recent times, royal music has sometimes featured more popular or contemporary genres.
Who's Malcolm McLaren ?

Malcolm McLaren (1946-2010) was an English musician, impresario, visual artist, and fashion designer, best known as the manager of the influential punk rock band, the Sex Pistols. He also managed and collaborated with other notable artists, including Adam and the Ants and Bow Wow Wow, and had a solo music career of his own. McLaren was known for his avant-garde and innovative approach to music and fashion, and is credited with helping to shape the punk and new wave movements of the 1970s and 1980s. In addition to his work in music, McLaren pursued a career in visual art and fashion design, and was involved in many other creative endeavors throughout his life.
right or wrong ? The manager of the Sex Pistols wanted the song ‘God Save the Queen’ to be criticized.

Right. Malcolm McLaren, the manager of the Sex Pistols, wanted the song "God Save the Queen" to be controversial and to criticize the monarchy and the British establishment. Released in 1977 during the Queen's Silver Jubilee celebrations, the song featured provocative lyrics that were seen as disrespectful by some people, and it was banned by the BBC. Despite this, "God Save the Queen" went on to become one of the most famous and influential songs of the punk rock movement, and it remains a symbol of the rebellion and anti-establishment sentiments that characterized the era.

The Sex Pistols were provocative towards people who were in favour of tradition and order.
right or wrong ?

Right. The Sex Pistols were known for their provocative, anti-establishment stance, and their music was frequently critical of traditional institutions and societal norms. They were seen as a symbol of rebellion against the establishment, capitalism, and conformity, and their lyrics and performances frequently challenged the values and expectations of mainstream society. In particular, the Sex Pistols were critical of the British monarchy and political system, and their music was seen as a reflection of the social and political upheaval of the late 1970s in Britain.
